channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI 투자 수익 창출? 전문가들이 말하는 ‘AI ROI에의 섣부른 집착이 위험한 이유’
item.description: IT 연구 및 자문 기업 포레스터(Forrester)에 따르면 많은 기업이 AI 프로젝트 시작 후 투자 수익(ROI)를 얻는 데 어려움을 겪고 있지만, 너무 빨리 높은 성과를 요구하면 위험할 수 있는 것으로 나타났다. 포레스터는 일부 IT 리더들이 빠른 가치 창출을 기대하기보다는 실험에 더 많은 시간을 주는 것이 중요하다는 점을 깨닫기 시작하면서, ROI에 집착하는 기업이 조기에 규모를 축소하게 될 수 있다고 예측했다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Blink and it’s here: Amazon’s affordable home security range finally arrives in Australia
item.description: Blink has been available in other markets, but Amazon’s budget alternative to Ring has arrived Down Under just in time for Prime Big Deal Days.
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYT Strands today — hints, answers and spangram for Wednesday, October 2 (game #213)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency
item.description: Do you know your true cloud costs? The post Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: This state is suing YouTube for fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: US port strike has CIOs prepping for supply chain implications
item.description: Dockworkers have launched a widespread strike at ports across the eastern US from Texas to Maine, with wages and automation topping their list of labor concerns. The stoppage, the first in decades, could cause price hikes and supply shortages, depending on how long it lasts, the AP reports.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Does your Halloween rager really need a Partiful invite?
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: California Governor's AI Oversight Veto: A Missed Opportunity?
item.description: Governor Gavin Newsom vetoes key AI oversight legislation, citing need for more comprehensive solution, but critics argue this is a missed opportunity to address growing AI risks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: General Motors' Cruise Faces $1.5 Million Fine Over Pedestrian Crash Cover-Up
item.description: General Motors' autonomous vehicle unit Cruise faces a $1.5 million fine for failing to disclose details about a serious crash involving a pedestrian in San Francisco, the NHTSA says.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Surgeons use PlayStation controller for long-distance endoscopy
item.description: Surgeons performed endoscopy on a pig 5,780 miles away using a video game controller. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on this groundbreaking experiment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Thousands of Verizon users report outages
item.description: Thousands of Verizon users across the country were experiencing service outages Monday morning. More than 100,000 Verizon users had reported outages as of midday Monday, according to Downdetector. The outage tracker’s map showed hotspots near Tampa, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Cruise ship surgery that slices giants for mega profits
item.description: A whopping 30 million people will have taken cruises in 2024, and some ships being built to keep up with this demand cost as much as $2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI bad actors 'not very Christian'
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DoD seeks to create ‘culture of learning’ through CDAO
item.description: Christopher Skaluba, the executive director in the DoD CDAO, said the office is creating tools to provide data-driven insights on AI workforce needs. The post DoD seeks to create ‘culture of learning’ through CDAO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine shows off capabilities of new drone-dropped, flamethrowing robotic war dogs: video
item.description: The Ukrainian Ministry of Defense shared a video of a robotic war dog being used in the field with a soldier, who was able to take advantage of the device's thermal imaging.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB engineer says carbon fiber hull from submersible showed signs of flaws
item.description: The carbon fiber hull of the experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreckage of the Titanic had imperfections dating to the manufacturing process and behaved differently after a loud bang was heard on one of the dives the year before the tragedy, an engineer with the National Transpo
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency Bill Advances in House: Key Points to Know
item.description: House lawmakers push for a cyber scholarship program, emphasizing skills-based training and government service.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: 10-year forecast shows growth in network architect jobs while sysadmin roles shrink
item.description: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) is forecasting a decade of growth for many – but not all – IT positions. The U.S. economy is expected to add 6.7 million jobs from 2023 to 2033, which equates to 4% job growth overall. The picture is rosier for IT occupations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rise of influencer skincare routines causing harm to young girls
item.description: "It is, however, a little frustrating as a professional in the industry to see kids overutilizing products that aren't necessary for their skin," said Old City Med Spa aesthetician Mahala Brien.
item.dc:creator: Lori Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Video game actors' union calls for strike against 'League of Legends'
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — “League of Legends” is caught in the middle of a dispute between Hollywood’s actors union and an audio company that provides voiceover services for the blockbuster online multiplayer game.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
